features.privacy_desc: All processing happens locally in your browser. Your AVIF images are never uploaded to any server — not even temporarily. What you view stays on your device.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: How do I open AVIF files on Windows 10 or 11?
faq.a1: Windows 10 and 11 require the 'AV1 Video Extension' from the Microsoft Store to open AVIF files natively. If you don't want to install anything, just use Avifkit — drop your file in and preview it instantly in your browser.
faq.q2: Can I view AVIF files on iPhone or iPad?
faq.a2: iOS 16 and later support AVIF natively in the Photos app. If you're on an older iOS version, you can still preview AVIF files using Avifkit in Safari.
faq.q3: Are my photos safe? Do they get uploaded?
faq.a3: Your files are 100% private. All previewing happens inside your browser using local processing — your images are never sent to any server. Not even temporarily.
faq.q4: Can I convert the AVIF file after viewing it?
faq.a4: Yes. After previewing, click 'Convert to JPG' or 'Convert to PNG' to instantly convert the file in your browser. No upload required for conversion either.
faq.q5: What file details does the viewer show?
faq.a5: The viewer displays the full-resolution image preview along with: image dimensions (width × height in pixels), file size, color depth, and file format type.
faq.q6: Which browsers can open AVIF files?
faq.a6: Chrome 85+, Firefox 93+, Opera 71+, Edge 121+, and Safari 16.4+ all support AVIF natively. Avifkit's viewer works in any of these browsers.
faq.q7: Can I preview multiple AVIF files at once?
faq.a7: Yes. You can drop multiple AVIF files at once and browse through them one by one. Each file shows its own full preview and metadata.
faq.q8: Why won't Photoshop open my AVIF file?
faq.a8: AVIF support was added in Adobe Photoshop 2024 (version 25.0+). Older versions can't open AVIF files. Use Avifkit to preview, or convert to JPG or PNG for compatibility with older Photoshop.
faq.a9: No. Avifkit is completely free with no account required. Open the page and start previewing.
faq.a10: AVIF (AV1 Image File Format) is a modern image format based on the AV1 video codec. It offers significantly better compression than JPG and WebP at the same visual quality — making files smaller without visible quality loss. Support has grown rapidly and it's now backed by Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and most modern phones.
